title: A003: Add minimal GitHub Actions CI workflow
summary: Clean, minimal GitHub Actions CI workflow with three parallel jobs (lint, test, test-integration). The implementation matches all 8 requirements exactly: SPDX header follows deploy-docs.yaml convention, triggers are correctly scoped to push-to-main and pull_request, permissions follow least-privilege (contents: read), concurrency group matches the mega-linter.yml pattern, lint uses golangci-lint-action@v9 with v2.10 pinned, and test/test-integration jobs use actions/setup-go@v5 with go-version-file: go.work. Reference documentation is thorough and accurate. No scope creep — excluded items (container builds, Helm, E2E, coverage, matrix) are absent.

tests_checklist.item: YAML syntax validates without errors (python yaml.safe_load)
Workflow structure matches GitHub Actions schema (name, on, permissions, concurrency, jobs)
All 16 test specifications from the plan are satisfied by the implementation
Infrastructure-only change — no executable code tests applicable (CI workflow is declarative YAML)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing workflow conventions (SPDX header, quoted 'on', ubuntu-latest, .yaml extension)
File is 47 lines — minimal, no unnecessary complexity
No code duplication beyond necessary per-job boilerplate (checkout + setup-go)
Clear step naming (Run unit tests, Run integration tests)
No dead code or commented-out sections

security_checklist.item: Top-level permissions restricted to contents: read (least privilege)
No job-level permission overrides that could escalate access
No hardcoded secrets, tokens, or credentials
Third-party actions pinned to major versions (@v4, @v5, @v9) — acceptable for well-known actions

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal — single file, 3 jobs, no matrix, no complex conditionals
Delegates build logic to Makefile targets (separation of concerns)
Uses go.work for Go version (consistent with workspace layout from CC-0001)
No excluded scope items present (container builds, Helm, E2E, coverage, matrix, branch protection)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No premature abstractions or reusable workflow components
No code coverage thresholds (YAGNI per scope)
No matrix builds per operator (Makefile handles iteration)
No branch protection configuration (repo setting, not workflow)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Each job fails independently on error (make exits non-zero on test failure)
golangci-lint-action reports lint failures as check failures

defensive_checklist.item: Permissions locked to read-only at top level
Concurrency group prevents resource waste on superseded runs

suggested_improvements: D2 (minor): Consider pinning third-party actions to full SHA refs (e.g., actions/checkout@<sha>) instead of major version tags for supply-chain hardening. This is a project-wide decision and not specific to this workflow — defer to when a security hardening pass is planned.

D1 (minor): The pull_request trigger includes `branches: [main]` which restricts CI to PRs targeting main only. This is sensible for the current repo layout but differs slightly from the plan wording ('pull_request' without branch filter). No action needed — the implementation is arguably more defensive.
next_steps: Merge CC-0001 (Go workspace, Makefile, .golangci.yml) to main before this workflow can pass — the CI depends on those artifacts
After merge, verify the workflow runs successfully on a test PR to confirm end-to-end functionality

detected_patterns.name: GitHub Actions workflow SPDX header and structure convention
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a new GitHub Actions workflow file to the repository
detected_patterns.description: All workflow YAML files use the .yaml extension and start with the SPDX license header (Copyright 2026 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company, Apache-2.0) followed by a blank comment line and a --- YAML document separator. The trigger key is quoted as '"on"' to prevent YAML boolean interpretation. Top-level permissions are restricted to contents: read (least privilege). The concurrency group pattern is ${{ github.ref }}-${{ github.workflow }} with cancel-in-progress: true. All jobs use runs-on: ubuntu-latest.

examples.code:
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright 2026 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
---
name: CI

"on":
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.ref }}-${{ github.workflow }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
